ChatBase Product Documentation AI Chatbot-as-a-Service Platform Version 1.0 | 2025 | Confidential 1. Product Overview ChatBase is a Chatbot-as-a-Service (CBaaS) platform that allows any website owner to embed a fully functional AI chatbot on their website with a single HTML script tag. The platform is powered by Google Gemini and provides a complete SaaS experience including user registration, API key management, widget customization, chat analytics, and a tiered subscription model. The core idea is simple: website owners should not need to understand AI APIs, server infrastructure, or machine learning to add an intelligent chatbot to their site. ChatBase handles all of that complexity behind the scenes. 1.1 Core Value Proposition 1.2 Who Is It For? ChatBase targets three main customer segments: Small business owners and bloggers who want to engage visitors without hiring developers Digital agencies managing multiple client websites who need centralized chatbot control SaaS companies and e-commerce stores that need 24/7 automated visitor support 1.3 How It Works — User Journey User registers on the ChatBase platform and creates an account A unique secret API key is automatically generated for their account User embeds a single <script> tag containing their API key into their website Visitors on the website see a chat widget and can start conversations Each message routes through the ChatBase server, is processed by Gemini, and returned to the visitor All conversations, stats, and usage are tracked and displayed in the owner's dashboard 2. System Architecture ChatBase uses a hub-and-spoke architecture. The ChatBase backend server sits at the center and is the only component that communicates with the Gemini API. This design means all security, billing, and plan enforcement happens in one controlled place. 2.1 Request Flow 2.2 Architecture Diagram (Text) Key architectural principle: The ChatBase server is the ONLY entity that ever holds or uses the Gemini API key. It is stored exclusively in server environment variables and never transmitted to any client. 2.3 Component Responsibilities 3. Development Phases The platform is built in four sequential phases. Each phase delivers a fully functional milestone that can be shipped and tested independently before the next phase begins. 4. Technology Stack ChatBase is built on a modern JavaScript stack with cloud-native services, chosen for developer velocity, global availability, and low operational overhead. 4.1 Frontend 4.2 Backend 4.3 AI & Services 4.4 Deployment 5. API Flow & Plan Enforcement Every chat request from a visitor passes through a multi-step validation pipeline on the ChatBase server before Gemini is ever called. This pipeline is where all plan limits are enforced in real time. 5.1 Request Validation Pipeline Receive request — Widget sends { apiKey, message, sessionId } via HTTPS POST to /api/chat Validate API key — Look up key in MongoDB. If not found, return 401 Unauthorized Check domain — Verify the request Origin header is in the user's allowed domains list. If not, return 403 Forbidden Check billing cycle — If current date exceeds billingCycleStart + 30 days, reset messageCount to 0 and update cycle date Check

key in MongoDB. If not found, return 401 Unauthorized Check domain — Verify the request Origin header is in the user's allowed domains list. If not, return 403 Forbidden Check billing cycle — If current date exceeds billingCycleStart + 30 days, reset messageCount to 0 and update cycle date Check message limit — Compare user.messageCount against plan limit. If exceeded, return 429 Limit Reached Call Gemini API — Forward the sanitized message to Gemini using the server's own API key Log conversation — Save the full exchange to MongoDB conversations collection Increment counter — Add 1 to user.messageCount in the users collection Return response — Send Gemini's reply back to the widget for display 5.2 Plan Limits Reference 5.3 Monthly Message Counter Reset Each user document in MongoDB stores a billingCycleStart date. On every API request, the server runs the following check: 5.4 Website/Domain Enforcement When a user adds a website in their dashboard, the domain is saved to their domains array in MongoDB. When a chat request arrives, the server reads the HTTP Origin header and checks if that domain exists in the user's domains array. The enforcement rules are: Starter plan: Maximum 1 domain in the domains array. Adding a 2nd domain is blocked at the dashboard level Pro plan: Maximum 5 domains. Requests from any 6th domain are rejected even if added Enterprise plan: No limit. All domains are accepted If a user downgrades from Pro to Starter, they are prompted to remove domains until only 1 remains before the downgrade completes 5.5 Multi-Country Operation ChatBase operates globally without any special configuration. Since it is a web-based API: MongoDB Atlas clusters can be deployed in US, Europe, or Asia — select the region closest to your primary user base The Node.js backend on Railway is globally reachable over HTTPS from any country Google Gemini API is available internationally — no geographic restrictions A user in Pakistan and one in Germany both simply hit the same HTTPS endpoint — there is no location-specific logic required 6. Database Schema ChatBase uses three core MongoDB collections. All collections are indexed on the fields used in the API hot path for fast lookups. 6.1 Users Collection 6.2 Conversations Collection 6.3 Subscriptions Collection 7. Security Model Security is built into every layer of the platform. The following measures protect both the platform operators and the website owners who use ChatBase. 7.1 API Key Security Each API key is generated using Node.js crypto.randomBytes() — cryptographically secure and unique Keys are stored in plaintext in MongoDB (they are not passwords — they are public credentials for widget embedding) Users can regenerate their key at any time. The old key is immediately invalidated The server's own Gemini API key is stored only in environment variables and is never transmitted to any client 7.2 Authentication Dashboard users authenticate with email + password. Passwords are hashed with bcrypt at 12 salt rounds Successful login returns a signed JWT with 7-day expiration All dashboard API routes require a

own Gemini API key is stored only in environment variables and is never transmitted to any client 7.2 Authentication Dashboard users authenticate with email + password. Passwords are hashed with bcrypt at 12 salt rounds Successful login returns a signed JWT with 7-day expiration All dashboard API routes require a valid JWT in the Authorization header The embedded widget does NOT use JWT — it uses only the API key for authentication 7.3 Domain Allowlisting Every inbound chat request's HTTP Origin header is checked against the user's registered domains list Requests from unregistered domains are rejected with 403 Forbidden before any Gemini call is made This prevents API key theft — even if someone copies a key from a page source, requests from their domain will be blocked 7.4 Rate Limiting & Abuse Prevention Per-API-key rate limiting (Phase 4): maximum 10 requests per 10 seconds per key Message length is capped at 1,000 characters to prevent runaway token usage All inputs are sanitized to prevent prompt injection attacks on the Gemini API Cloudflare DDoS protection filters malicious traffic at the network edge 7.5 Data Privacy Conversation data belongs to the website owner and is stored in their namespace in MongoDB No conversation content is used to train any AI model Data is encrypted at rest by MongoDB Atlas and in transit via HTTPS/TLS Chat log retention limits per plan ensure old data is purged (7 days for Starter, 90 days for Pro) 8. Pricing Plans ChatBase offers three subscription tiers to accommodate users from individual bloggers to large enterprises. All plans include core chatbot functionality. Higher tiers unlock more messages, more websites, and longer data retention. 9. Deployment Strategy ChatBase uses a fully cloud-native deployment with separate services for frontend, backend, and database. This separation ensures each layer can be scaled, updated, and monitored independently. 9.1 Frontend — Vercel React app deployed to Vercel's global CDN for sub-100ms load times worldwide Automatic deploys triggered on every push to the main branch via GitHub integration Environment variable: VITE_API_URL pointing to the backend server URL Custom domain with automatic SSL certificate provisioning 9.2 Backend — Railway / Render Node.js + Express server deployed as a containerized service Auto-scaling handles traffic spikes without manual intervention All secrets (GEMINI_API_KEY, MONGO_URI, JWT_SECRET, STRIPE_SECRET) stored in Railway's encrypted secrets vault Health check endpoint at GET /health for uptime monitoring Zero-downtime deploys via Railway's rolling deploy strategy 9.3 Database — MongoDB Atlas M10+ dedicated cluster for production (M0 free tier for development only) Replica set with 3 nodes for high availability and automatic failover Automated daily backups with point-in-time recovery Deploy cluster in the region closest to the Railway server (e.g., AWS us-east-1) IP allowlist configured to accept connections only from the Railway server IP 9.4 Critical Environment Variables 10. Dashboard Features The ChatBase dashboard is a React SPA (Single Page Application) with a persistent sidebar navigation. Every page is designed for clarity — website owners with no technical background should understand their data at a glance.

accept connections only from the Railway server IP 9.4 Critical Environment Variables 10. Dashboard Features The ChatBase dashboard is a React SPA (Single Page Application) with a persistent sidebar navigation. Every page is designed for clarity — website owners with no technical background should understand their data at a glance. 10.1 Pages Overview 11. Future Roadmap The following features are planned for post-launch iterations based on user feedback and market demand. Short Term (v1.1 – v1.3) Custom system prompts — allow owners to define the bot's persona and knowledge File upload support — let visitors upload PDFs or images for the bot to analyze Multi-language widget — auto-detect visitor language and respond accordingly Email digest reports — weekly summary of chat activity sent to owners Webhook integrations — send conversation data to Slack, Zapier, or custom endpoints Medium Term (v2.0) Knowledge base integration — owners can upload documents that the bot learns from Human handoff — escalate to a live agent when the bot cannot answer Team accounts — multiple team members sharing one ChatBase account White-label option — agencies can rebrand the platform for their own clients Mobile app — iOS and Android app for monitoring chats on the go Long Term (v3.0+) Multi-model support — choose between Gemini, GPT-4, Claude, or custom models CRM integrations — native connectors for HubSpot, Salesforce, Intercom Voice chatbot — audio-based widget for accessibility and hands-free interaction E-commerce mode — widget connected to product catalog for shopping assistance Appendix: Glossary End of Document Metric | Value Build Phases | 4 sequential phases Pricing Tiers | 3 (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) Tech Stack | React · Node.js · MongoDB · Gemini API Deployment | Vercel (frontend) + Railway (backend) Target Market | Website owners worldwide Core Value | 1 script tag to AI chatbot Feature | Description One-line embed | A single <script> tag activates the chatbot on any website Managed AI | Gemini API is fully managed — users never touch it directly Real-time analytics | Every conversation is logged and surfaced in the dashboard Plan-based limits | Message and website limits enforced automatically per plan Widget customization | Bot name, color, theme, position all customizable with live preview Global access | Works for website owners in any country, any timezone Step | Component | Action 1 | Website Visitor | Types a message in the chat widget on the host website 2 | Widget JS | Sends message + API key to ChatBase backend via HTTPS POST 3 | ChatBase Server | Validates API key, checks domain allowlist, checks plan limits 4 | ChatBase Server | Calls Gemini API with the user's message 5 | Gemini API | Returns AI-generated response 6 | ChatBase Server | Logs conversation to MongoDB, increments message counter 7 | Widget JS | Displays Gemini's response to the website visitor Flow: Website Visitor → Embedded Widget (JS) → ChatBase API Server → [MongoDB Atlas] & [Gemini API] → Response back to Widget Component | Technology | Variable | Location | Description GEMINI_API_KEY | Backend only | Google Gemini API key — NEVER expose to frontend MONGO_URI | Backend only | MongoDB Atlas connection string with credentials JWT_SECRET | Backend only | Secret key for signing JWT tokens — min 32 chars STRIPE_SECRET | Backend only | Stripe secret key for server-side billing operations STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET | Backend only | Stripe webhook signing secret VITE_API_URL | Frontend build | Public URL of the backend API server Page | Route | Purpose Dashboard | /dashboard | Overview stats, recent conversations, widget status, activity chart API Key | /api-key | View, copy, regenerate secret API key; embed code snippet; usage stats Widget Setup | /widget-setup | 3-step wizard: copy script, platform guide, verify installation Customization | /customization | Bot name, color, theme, position with

| /dashboard | Overview stats, recent conversations, widget status, activity chart API Key | /api-key | View, copy, regenerate secret API key; embed code snippet; usage stats Widget Setup | /widget-setup | 3-step wizard: copy script, platform guide, verify installation Customization | /customization | Bot name, color, theme, position with real-time widget preview Chat Logs | /chat-logs | Searchable conversation table with click-to-expand message viewer Analytics | /analytics | Bar charts, top pages, peak hours, satisfaction ratings Settings | /settings | Profile, password change, notification toggles, domain allowlist Plans | /plans | Pricing tiers, upgrade/downgrade flow, invoice history Term | Definition API Key | A unique secret string assigned to each user that authenticates their widget requests CBaaS | Chatbot-as-a-Service — a managed service model for deploying AI chatbots Gemini API | Google's large language model API used to generate AI responses Widget | The embeddable JavaScript chat interface that appears on the owner's website billingCycleStart | The date stored per user that determines when their monthly message count resets JWT | JSON Web Token — a signed token used to authenticate dashboard users Domain allowlist | The list of website domains permitted to use a user's API key messageCount | Running counter of messages sent by a user's visitors in the current billing cycle DXA | Document eXtensible Architecture — unit of measurement in Word documents (1440 = 1 inch) CDN | Content Delivery Network — servers distributed globally to serve files quickly

DIGICHAT AI-Powered Customer Support Platform Project Report • Sales & Leadership Presentation 01 Executive Summary DigiChat is a fully custom-built AI chatbot SaaS platform by DigiCare Products. It allows any business to deploy a smart, branded customer support widget on their website in under 5 minutes no technical knowledge required. The bot is trained on each business's own data products, prices, policies and contact info so it gives accurate, specific answers to every customer question, 24 hours a day. 02 What is DigiChat? DigiChat gives every business their own AI chatbot trained exclusively on their data. Visitors on the business website get instant, accurate answers without waiting for a human agent. 03 How It Works The entire journey from signup to a live chatbot takes under 10 minutes: 04 Technology Stack 05 How Customers Train Their Bot No technical knowledge required. Two simple options: 06 What Has Been Delivered 07 Future Scope & Roadmap DigiChat is built to scale. Below is the planned growth roadmap: 08 Market Opportunity Any business with a website is a potential customer. The platform is specifically designed for the South Asian SME market. DigiChat is live and ready for customers. digicareproducts.com/digichat DigiCare Products — Confidential Prepared by | DigiCare Products Dev Team Date | 11 March 2026 Platform | digicareproducts.com/digichat Status | ✅ Live & Operational 14,400 AI Requests / Day | 6+ Supported Platforms | < 5 min Setup Time | 24 / 7 Uptime 🤖 | AI Chat Widget | One line of code to embed on any website. Works around the clock automatically. 🧠 | Custom Training | Bot is trained on the business's own products, prices, FAQs and policies not generic info. 📊 | Live Analytics | Owners see all conversations, message volume and visitor trends in real time. 🎨 | Full Branding | Custom bot name, colour, theme and welcome message per business. 🔒 | Isolated Data | Every business has their own private knowledge base data is never shared or mixed. ⚡ | Sub-Second Replies | Powered by Groq LPU hardware responses delivered in under one second. Customer Journey 1. Sign up & receive API key 2. Fill business info form OR upload a file 3. Copy one embed script to their website 4. Widget goes live instantly 5.
